title: Demonstration of high-fidelity entangled logical qubits using transmons | Nature Communications
why:
- The study proposes and implements a method that uses dynamical decoupling (DD) with quantum error correction (QEC) codes to drastically suppress logical errors.
- This hybrid QEC-NDD (Normalizer Dynamical Decoupling) strategy is capable of handling arbitrary weight errors.
- Experiments using IBM transmon devices and the [[4, 2, 2]] code showed performance that significantly exceeds the capabilities of using either QEC code or DD alone.
- The method enables detection of logical errors affecting logically encoded Bell states, with demonstrated high-fidelity entangled logical qubits.
- The achieved fidelities are beyond-breakeven, significantly exceeding those of unprotected entangled qubits in the same setting.
